title: oxycarpus celebica
intro: Oxycarpus celebica, also known as Celebes oak or Borneo oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the island of Sulawesi in Indonesia. The bark of Oxycarpus celebica contains tannins and other bioactive compounds, which have been used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Oxycarpus celebica has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off bacterial and fungal infections. In herbal preparations, Oxycarpus celebica is often combined with other plants to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to support overall health and well-being.
